ActionItems: Confirm Q3 EU-VGAS work-package retainer schedule with Marco
Review field-travel cost assumptions with Jón before year-end
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Back-of-napkin look at whether the hazard-advisory side practice covers its own field and admin costs each month. Pre-review with Jón Stefánsson (Norðan Accounting). Amounts in ISK.

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. MembershipDues stands in for retained advisory-day clients; DropIns for one-off briefings. Revisit once 2026 actuals close with Jón.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is roughly 3 advisory-day equivalents per month at current cost structure. At 4 retained clients plus occasional briefings there is comfortable headroom even in quiet field months.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs 425,000 ISK + 60,000 ISK added field-travel = 485,000 ISK. At 180,000 ISK/advisory day, 485,000 / 180,000 = 2.7 → 3 advisory days minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at current client load.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Field-travel costs rise if summer highland campaign extends

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Unlikely but stress-tested ahead of Katla readiness deadline.
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case, added instrument servicing plus extended charter
